title: Gentle Silky Northern Thai Mushrooms Khanom Krok
description: A vegan-friendly, umami-rich Northern Thai dish featuring elderflower and mushrooms in a silky khanom krok form, perfect for a brunch mood. This recipe combines the unique flavors of Thailand with the delicate taste of elderflower.

ingredients.name: mushrooms
ingredients.notes: mixed varieties such as cremini, shiitake, and oyster

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the rice flour, water, and salt to make a smooth batter.
Heat the peanut oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the crumbled tempeh to the skillet and cook until golden brown, about 5 minutes.
Add the mixed mushrooms to the skillet and cook until they release their moisture and start to brown, about 5 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together the coconut milk and elderflower syrup.
Pour the coconut milk mixture into the skillet with the mushrooms and tempeh, and stir to combine.
Using a 1/4 cup measuring cup, scoop the batter into the skillet and cook until the bottom is golden brown, about 3 minutes.
Loosen the khanom krok with a spatula and flip it over, cooking for an additional 2 minutes.
Serve the khanom krok warm, garnished with fresh herbs and a drizzle of elderflower syrup.
